questions.question: State the value of the Avogadro constant, Nₐ, including its unit.
Calculate the amount, in mol, of water molecules in 36.0 g of water (H₂O). Give your answer to 3 significant figures. [M(H₂O) = 18.0 g mol⁻¹]
Explain what is meant by the term 'molar mass' and describe how it is related to relative molecular mass. Include an example using ammonia (NH₃).
State the volume, in cm³, occupied by one mole of any gas at RTP (room temperature and pressure).
Calculate the amount, in mol, of carbon dioxide gas in 1200 cm³ at RTP.
Describe what is meant by the term 'amount of substance'. Include in your answer the unit used and how it relates to the Avogadro constant.
State the mass, in g, of one mole of calcium atoms. [Ar(Ca) = 40.1]
Calculate the number of chlorine molecules in 0.250 mol of Cl₂. [Nₐ = 6.02 × 10²³ mol⁻¹]

A student says 'one mole of iron and one mole of sulfur have the same mass.' Explain why this statement is incorrect and calculate the ratio of the mass of one mole of iron to one mole of sulfur. [Ar(Fe) = 55.8; Ar(S) = 32.1]
State the conditions for the molar gas volume of 24,000 cm³ mol⁻¹ to apply.
Calculate the mass of 0.500 mol of magnesium sulfate (MgSO₄). [M(MgSO₄) = 120 g mol⁻¹]
Explain why 1 mol of helium gas and 1 mol of nitrogen gas occupy the same volume at RTP despite having very different molar masses. Include the value of this volume in your answer.
State the name of the quantity measured in mol dm⁻³.
Calculate the amount, in mol, of potassium atoms in 7.82 g of potassium. [Ar(K) = 39.1]
Describe how you would use the equation n = m / M to find the number of formula units in a 2.00 g sample of sodium chloride (NaCl). Show all steps. [M(NaCl) = 58.5 g mol⁻¹; Nₐ = 6.02 × 10²³ mol⁻¹]
